title: Have "schedule option" on Email & Activities like the one on Email
excerpt: Have “schedule option” on Email & Activities like the one on Email when sending email to somebody. So we can have an option to write an email today and schedule to be send next day in the morning

posts.author: ofuks001 (Omara Fuks)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have “schedule option” on Email & Activities like the one on Email when sending email to somebody. So we can have an option to write an email today and schedule to be send next day in the morning

image786×291 21.8 KB

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @ofuks001!

Thank you for that feature request.

I will make sure to pass it to our R&D team so it can be considered

Cheers,
-Matias

posts.author: slothzero (Peter Freed)
posts.cookedHtml:
I asked for this over a year ago and you guys said the same thing…

An alternate idea would simply to allow a date/time field to control a status column precisely (down to the minute), and then have that status column trigger the email or SMS (through Twilio) to be sent. Right now there is a craziness in which you have to choose what hour (and only the hour – no minutes allowed!) to have an automation take effect. Soooooo much easier to allow the date/time to control the status change. EG the automation would read “when date/time arrives, change status to send” and then trigger the email or sms to be sent when the status changes to send.

I hope you guys realize all major email systems allow scheduled sending… this is not some kind of minor feature you are missing… millions upon millions of people want to schedule emails/sms

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @slothzero !

Thank you for the feedback.

I will share this with our team so that it can be taken into account

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: traffik-world (Jon Agostini)
posts.cookedHtml:
Guys,
I don’t understand why this is not a feature yet!
Is this not important enough or too difficult?

From an Excel spreadsheet, I can send mass targeted scheduled emails with attachments to my contact list. Emails will be personalized and delivered within business hours according to the time zone. From there, I managed to translate the logic into AirTable and I can do it from there.
The only reason I stick to Monday is that I don’t have the time to migrate to AirTable which 20$ per month vs 50$ for Monday CRM.

The lack of features surrounding muting notifications on mobile makes this doubly worse. It is terrible for productivity.

posts.author: arkitrave (Eric Shepherd)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another vote for this - Monday is built on scheduling things! It’s pretty disappointing that this isn’t even acknowledged as a future roadmap item yet. I can compensate for the poor HTML email creation by using an external tool to make my HTML emails, but when I go to send, I have to send in real time. Please put this critical feature in the roadmap.

posts.author: ChelseaBolen (Chelsea Bolen)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another vote as well. I often work late nights and would rather schedule something for a reasonable time later in the day than send an email dated at 2:03am. It’s weird that it’s not a feature already.

posts.author: BigHornDirectory (Angie Huffman)
posts.cookedHtml: I just moved out of work management program in Monday to the CRM program and pretty sure I Lost my delay send option. That makes no sense. In the CRM I need this feature more.

posts.author: SkyJohnson (Skyley Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m not sure why it’s taken 2+ years for this feature to still not exist.

We can do a delay send when using the mass emails function, so why can’t we just take the same template and apply it to regular one off emails?

Big oversight here because this function is needed more in one offs than it is for mass emails.

posts.author: stratcann (Heather)
posts.cookedHtml: I am really in need of this function and it’s now June 2025. Has there been any progress on adding the option to write an email and schedule the delivery date and time? I can do it on all of my other platforms, I shocked it’s not an option on my paid Monday.com account - unless I’m missing something?

posts.author: carlos360F (Carlos)

posts.author: adamph (Adam)

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone! Thank you all for following up on this feature request. We really appreciate your continued feedback. If you are sending mass emails through Emails & Activities, there is a native scheduling feature via the dropdown menu (Mass emailing > Schedule emails):

image1400×1180 73.8 KB

However, if your team is looking to map out consecutive singular/mass emails to your contacts, I recommend checking out the Sequences feature in the CRM product:

https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/20666311273874-Sequences

Beyond emails, this feature also allows you to integrate other activities and manual actions within your automated sequences. For example, after an initial email, if your team follows up with a call, you can build that step into your sequence and set conditions to specify when this next step will occur. You can read the linked article above for more detail!

Please note that the Sequences feature is not found within Email & Activities. You can review or create new sequences directly on your board via the highlighted button below, or directly from your workspace. Selecting “Sequences” from your workspace gives you the option to review and edit your sequences across all of your CRM boards.

image2990×394 114 KB

Let me know if you have any follow-up questions!

posts.author: adamph (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml: It is nice to see there is progress towards this feature. But for us (and I’m sure many others), we simply need an option to schedule/send later an email reply from say Monday Service or on a Deal on Monday CRM. It seems to be such a core feature that everything from Gmail to Pipedrive is offering within its basic products. As we are becoming more globalised, it is so important to be able to send a response to an email at an hour the recipient is likely to see it, regardless of which timezone we are in.

posts.author: stratcann (Heather)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, it is a required function for many businesses.

posts.author: adamph (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we get an update from Monday on this? Since moving from Pipedrive this is a massive missing feature on Monday.
